user_query:
I'm coordinating several upcoming field survey teams and need a small, reproducible geospatial enrichment pipeline for our planning spreadsheet. Please implement and run a Python script that downloads official Natural Earth boundary/coastline data, enriches our planned site coordinates, and produces a concise summary plus a draft email for the team.

Requirements:
1) Script implementation and invocation
- Implement a CLI script at scripts/enrich_sites.py that I can run as:
  python scripts/enrich_sites.py --input input/survey_sites.csv --outdir outputs --datadir data
- The script must create outdir and datadir if they do not exist.
- The script must programmatically download the following Natural Earth 1:10m datasets from the official Natural Earth website (no interactive logins or API keys):
  • Admin 0 – Countries (title typically includes "Admin 0 - Countries", 1:10m scale)
  • Admin 1 – States and Provinces (1:10m scale)
  • Coastline (1:10m scale)
  Save the original zipped downloads under datadir/, unpack them, and use the unpacked files for processing. Record the dataset titles you used exactly as listed by the official source in the manifest (see item 4).

2) Data validation and de-duplication
- Read input/survey_sites.csv with columns: site_id, latitude, longitude, planned_date (YYYY-MM-DD), team.
- Identify and write out-of-range coordinates (latitude not in [-90,90] or longitude not in [-180,180]) to outputs/invalid_sites.csv with an added column reason set to "invalid_lat_lon". Do not process invalid rows further.
- For remaining rows, de-duplicate by site_id: keep the row with the earliest planned_date and write all discarded duplicates to outputs/duplicates.csv (include all original columns plus a reason column set to "duplicate_site_id").

3) Geospatial enrichment
- For each remaining valid, unique site:
  • Determine onshore/offshore by testing if the point falls within any country polygon (Admin 0). Onshore points are those inside a country polygon.
  • Assign country as the name field from the Admin 0 dataset (leave blank if offshore).
  • Assign admin1 as the state/province name from the Admin 1 dataset for onshore points (leave blank if none intersects or if offshore).
  • Compute distance_km_to_coast as the geodesic distance in kilometers from the point to the nearest coastline feature from the Coastline dataset. For onshore points, set distance_km_to_coast to 0.00. For offshore points, compute the positive geodesic distance and round to two decimals.
- Output enriched rows to outputs/enriched_sites.csv with exactly these columns and order:
  site_id, planned_date, team, latitude, longitude, country, admin1, onshore, distance_km_to_coast
  where onshore is true/false.

4) Manifest and summary
- Write a manifest to outputs/data_sources.yaml with fields:
  datasets: a list of objects with { title, scale: "1:10m", source_org: "Natural Earth", download_datetime: ISO8601 UTC, local_path: relative path to extracted main shapefile or directory }
  input: { file: "input/survey_sites.csv", valid_rows_after_dedup: <int> }
- Create outputs/summary_by_country.csv summarizing onshore points only with columns:
  country, site_count, teams, avg_distance_km_to_coast
  where teams is a comma-separated list of unique team names for that country, and avg_distance_km_to_coast is the mean of distance_km_to_coast for that country rounded to two decimals (should be 0.00 for onshore points).

5) Draft email
- Draft a concise email to the field leads in outputs/email_draft.txt that includes:
  • Total number of valid unique sites processed
  • A table-like section with the first five lines of summary_by_country.csv
  • The relative paths to enriched_sites.csv and summary_by_country.csv
  • A short note that boundaries/coastlines were sourced from Natural Earth (1:10m) and the processing date

Constraints and notes:
- Use only offline downloads of the official Natural Earth datasets identified by their titles and 1:10m scale; do not use web geocoding services or interactive portals.
- The script must run end-to-end locally, perform the downloads if data are missing, and produce all outputs exactly at the specified paths.
- Please run your script to generate the outputs in the workspace so I can review them immediately.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
